title: Spicy Kenyan Vermicelli Stir-Fry
description: A sour, chewy one-pot dish featuring vermicelli pasta with a spicy kick in a simple Korean-inspired sauce.

ingredients.name: 1 cup of diced tofu or tempeh
2 tablespoons of soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free option)
1 tablespoon of coconut aminos (tamari if vegan/vegetarian)
3 tablespoons of red pepper flakes or to taste

instructions: Heat the oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and finely chopped onion. Stir-fry until softened but not browned.
Add the soaked and drained vermicelli, followed by the soy sauce (tamari for vegan/vegetarian), coconut aminos (or tamari), lime juice, red pepper flakes, and tofu or tempeh.
Stir everything together thoroughly to combine all ingredients evenly.
Reduce heat to low-medium and cook until the vermicelli is tender but still chewy, about 5-7 minutes. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Once done, remove from heat and garnish with fresh cilantro if desired.
Serve hot as a main dish or side.
